Global Data Centres and Electricity Consumption

Data centres for artificial intelligence and other forms of computing account for a small share of worldwide electricity consumption, according to estimates prepared by the International Energy Agency (IEA) and other researchers.

Consumption is growing very rapidly but from a low base and the extra demand will be dwarfed by increases from industrial users, electric vehicles, domestic appliances and airconditioning over the next decade.

From a global perspective, increased electricity demand from data centres is likely to have only a moderate impact on prices, service and reliability for other users.

But data centres are strongly clustered in a small number of countries, and within them a small number of regions, a pattern that is expected to persist.

In these regions, data centres account for a high and growing share of consumption, having a much greater impact on other users.

Data centres and artificial intelligence are expected to pose a relatively modest challenge for the global energy system in the future.

But managing their local impacts on availability, cost and reliability for other users is already proving a significant challenge ...

Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: Escalation continues.

The US and Iran continue to bomb each other, but no tankers have been attacked, at least not any that have been publicly reported.

Mainstream media in the past 48 hours really started to pick up on the notion that the oil shuttling service through the Oman lane is slowing down. Based on satellite data, it is not completely dead yet, but how much are the shipowners willing to risk it? 

Well, they are having a hard time getting crew members to agree, and a few have already reported that the captains have refused transit.

In the meantime, the Iran lane is getting all the traffic with AIS turned on. No visible tanker transits through the Oman lane, as expected.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.

We are so far detached from reality that it is hard to explain with words. I’ve been at a loss for words for weeks now, reading sellside reports about how the Strait of Hormuz will return to normal by the end of July. Well, we are two weeks away now. Does it look like things are going back to normal?

EIA weekly petroleum status: US crude inventories fell by 1.7 million barrels, while a further 3 million barrels were released from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR), taking the cumulative post-war drawdown to almost 100 million barrels. Gasoline stocks declined by 1.5 million barrels, with Gulf Coast inventories falling to their lowest level since 2017, highlighting continued regional tightness during the peak summer driving season. Distillate inventories, however, rose by 4.6 million barrels, providing some welcome relief to the segment of the refined products market that has experienced the greatest supply stress in recent months. 
Elsewhere, inventories at the Cushing delivery hub climbed back above 20 million barrels. US crude exports increased to 3.7 million bpd but remained below the one-year average of 4.2 million bpd and well below the record 6.4 million bpd reached in May. 
Domestic crude production was unchanged, while four-week average implied demand for both gasoline and distillates eased, broadly in line with normal seasonal trends.

Just in: Chip stocks are in the doldrums, San Disk and Micron plummet, technology giants come to the rescue, Apple rises 4%, Google rises more than 3%, Chinese concept stocks shine, Alibaba soars.

*The three major stock indexes closed higher, with the Nasdaq rising more than 0.6%; * Mid- and long-term U.S. bond yields fell, with the 2-year U.S. bond at 4.13%; *Paypal soared over 17% as the company received an acquisition offer.

Boosted by the continued cooling of inflation data and a bright start to the second-quarter earnings season, Wall Street's three major stock indexes closed higher across the board. As of the close, the Dow rose 150.37 points, or 0.29%, to 52,658.64 points, the Nasdaq rose 0.62%, to 26,269.23 points, and the S&P 500 rose 0.38% to 7,572.40 points.

There was a clear repositioning of funds on the market: holdings of high-priced semiconductor stocks were reduced and allocations were made to large technology leaders with stable profits. Amazon, Meta, and Alphabet rose more than 3%, and Apple rose 4.01%.

The chip sector weakened across the board, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index falling 2.2%, Intel falling 4.43%, AMD falling 3.46%, and Applied Materials falling 2.73%. The performance of memory chip targets was sluggish, with Micron Technology, SanDisk and Western Digital falling more than 8%.

SK Hynix fell 9.00%, failing to extend its strength from the previous trading day. Financial stocks strengthened due to performance boost, with Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase rising more than 1%. PayPal rose 17.20%.

Payment giant Stripe and private equity firm Advent International jointly issued an acquisition offer for PayPal at US$60.50 per share, a premium of approximately 28% to the stock's closing price on Tuesday. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 2.92%, and Alibaba rose nearly 5%.

According to reports, Alibaba's large model Tongyi Qianwen will be integrated into Apple Intelligence as an AI capability to provide intelligent services to Chinese users. Pinduoduo rose 2.18%, while Baidu, NetEase and https://t.co/6YYIAsV2GU rose more than 1%. On Wednesday, the U.S.

Bureau of Labor Statistics released June PPI data, which unexpectedly fell 0.3% month-on-month. The market had expected it to be flat, and the year-on-year growth rate was still as high as 5.5%. Core PPI rose to 4.7% year-on-year, up from 4.6% in May.

Melissa Brown, global head of investment decision research at SimCorp, commented: "This data cannot completely rule out the option of raising interest rates. The policy target is 2% inflation. All current price indicators are still significantly higher than the target.

Today, the market is prone to excessive unilateral reactions to a single data." This PPI report follows the release of CPI data that weakened more than expected on Tuesday. Two inflation reports boosted market expectations: the Federal Reserve does not need to aggressively raise interest rates this year.

According to the CME FedWatch tool, the market has significantly lowered the probability of raising interest rates in July in the past two days, but traders are still pricing in a rate hike within the year; the market expects that at the end of the October interest rate meeting, the probability of a 25 basis point increase in the benchmark interest rate is about 60%.

The situation in the Middle East continues to be tense. According to CCTV News, on July 15, local time, the U.S. Central Command announced that at 15:00 Eastern Time, the U.S. military launched the second wave of strikes against Iran that day.

The strike targeted Iran's military capabilities used to threaten the free passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian Parliament Speaker Qalibaf issued a statement on the war and the latest domestic situation.

Qalibaf said that Iran has never welcomed war and will never welcome war, but in order to safeguard national security and interests, it must always be ready for war and must do its best. Qalibaf pointed out that Iran should also use diplomacy and negotiation to achieve and consolidate national interests.

The Federal Reserve released its Beige Book of Economic Conditions, stating that the U.S. economy has improved overall, with more jobs but no sharp rise in wages, which will not trigger a spiral of wage inflation. Inflation has cooled slightly, but pressure remains significant, making it difficult to be optimistic.

Federal Reserve Chairman Warsh continued to appear in the Senate to attend the hearing on the monetary policy report.

Warsh reiterated that the Federal Open Market Committee will be solely responsible for controlling inflation and will take action if necessary to bring inflation back to the 2% target range, but did not release any forward guidance signals on monetary policy.

He believes that the previous round of inflation was caused by past improper monetary policies and will not happen again during his term. He also emphasized that interest rate decisions will be completely independent and will not be interfered by political factors.

According to Wash's analysis, artificial intelligence is likely to push up prices in the short term, but with subsequent improvements in production efficiency, it will gradually offset the upward pressure on inflation and ease the pace of price increases. New York Fed President Williams said that the U.S.

economic growth is solid and the labor market remains stable, but inflation is still higher than the 2% policy target and must continue to be suppressed. He pointed out that the current tightening of monetary policy is just right to deal with inflation; he predicted that inflation will continue to slow down in the next few quarters.

Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook said the Fed is ready to take tightening action if inflation fails to fall quickly and sustainably. Mid- and long-term U.S. bond yields fell, with the benchmark 10-year U.S. bond falling 4 basis points to 4.55%, and the 2-year U.S.

bond, which is closely related to interest rate expectations, falling 6.3 basis points to 4.13%. International oil prices rose. The front-month contract of WTI crude oil rose 0.33% to US$79.60/barrel, and the front-month contract of Brent crude oil rose 0.26% to US$84.95/barrel. The precious metals market was under pressure.

COMEX gold futures for July delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange fell 0.42% to US$4,044.00 per ounce, and COMEX silver futures fell 2.83% to US$57.11 per barrel. (

Just in: Morgan Stanley's Q 2 revenue and profit hit new highs, stock trading revenue surged 69%.

Before the market opens on Wednesday (July 15) Eastern Time, Morgan Stanley announced a strong second-quarter report. Driven by its stock trading and investment banking business, the bank's second-quarter revenue and profits hit record highs.

The financial report shows that Morgan Stanley’s Q2 revenue was a record $21.35 billion, a year-on-year increase of 27%, higher than the market expectation of $19.64 billion; net profit increased by 58% year-on-year to $5.58 billion, with earnings per share (EPS) of $3.46, higher than analysts’ expectations of $2.94; Similar to Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase, the stock trading business has also become the main driving force for Morgan Stanley's strong performance growth.

In the second quarter of this year, Morgan Stanley's stock trading business revenue hit a record high of US$6.3 billion, approximately US$1.9 billion higher than analysts surveyed by StreetAccount expected, and a year-on-year increase of 69%.

Morgan Stanley said that the confrontation between the United States and Iran led to a sharp rise in oil prices, intensified global market volatility, and a significant increase in customer trading activities, driving the strong performance of the stock trading business; a large part of the growth in Q2 trading revenue came from Asian markets, including markets such as Hong Kong, India, Japan, and South Korea.

At the same time, Morgan Stanley’s fixed income trading business revenue increased by 13% year-on-year in Q2 to US$2.46 billion, basically in line with market expectations, mainly driven by the good performance of its credit trading business.

Driven by active IPO underwriting, M&A advisory business and related transactions, Morgan Stanley's investment banking revenue surged 58% year-on-year to US$2.44 billion, which was approximately US$270 million higher than analysts' expectations. Morgan Stanley served as one of the lead underwriters on the record-breaking IPO of Elon Musk’s SpaceX.

This listing transaction, with a fundraising scale of US$2 trillion and a market capitalization, is regarded as an important milestone in the recovery of the US IPO market.

Revenue from the company's largest wealth management business increased 14% year-on-year to US$8.86 billion, about US$146 million higher than market expectations, benefiting from an increase in asset size driven by the rise in the stock market, as well as growth in deposits and loans.

Revenue from the smallest asset management business increased by about 6% year-on-year to US$1.65 billion, mainly driven by rising asset values and basically in line with market expectations. Are the six major U.S. banks at their best? As Morgan Stanley announced its results, the second-quarter revenue and profits of the six major U.S.

banks exceeded analysts’ expectations. Some of the data are even so good that they are “exaggerated”. For example, JPMorgan Chase's annualized return on equity (ROE) reached 24%, while analysts had expected only 19%. Among them, Visa’s shareholding investment income provided additional help. Goldman Sachs' ROE also reached 24% this quarter.

"We are getting close to the best period, but we don't know how long it can last," JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said on a conference call with analysts on Tuesday. JP Morgan Chief Financial Officer Jeremy Barnum said: "The current market risk appetite has obviously increased, and we are just the beneficiary of this market environment." (

Just in: U.S. PPI in June is lower than expected, U.S. stock futures rise, ASML earnings report boosts chip stocks: What to watch tonight.

U.S. stock index futures edged higher on Wednesday, led by chip stocks. Europe's major indexes were mixed. (Source: Yingwei Finance) Data released before the market opened showed that the U.S. PPI in June increased by 5.5% year-on-year, and was expected to be 6.2%; it fell by 0.3% month-on-month, and was expected to be flat.

The core PPI in the United States in June increased by 4.7% year-on-year, and was expected to increase by 5.2%; it increased by 0.2% month-on-month, and was expected to increase by 0.4%. Driven by the semiconductor sector, Nasdaq 100 index futures led the rise in U.S.

stock futures, mainly due to the lithography machine giant ASML's outstanding quarterly report. ASML's new orders hit a record high and it raised its sales guidance for the second time this year, indicating that spending related to advanced chip manufacturing remains strong. As of press time, ASML's U.S.

stocks have risen by more than 3% before the market opens, Intel has risen by more than 2%, and AMD, Arm and Qualcomm have all risen by more than 1%. International oil prices rose, with Brent crude futures rising above $85 a barrel, after the U.S. Central Command said the U.S. military would launch more attacks on Iran. U.S.

inflation data released on Tuesday came in below market expectations, reinforcing investor expectations that the Federal Reserve will not need to raise interest rates significantly this year. According to the CME Group's FedWatch tool, the probability of a rate hike at the Fed's July meeting has dropped to 17% from 42% the day before.

However, the market still expects the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates later this year, with traders currently predicting a 63% chance of a 25 or 50 basis point increase in interest rates after the September meeting.

In terms of financial reporting season, BlackRock announced that its assets under management reached a record high in the second quarter; Morgan Stanley followed other large banks and announced strong quarterly results.

[Morgan Stanley’s net revenue in the second quarter was US$21.35 billion] On July 15, Morgan Stanley announced its second quarter 2026 performance report.

Net revenue in the second quarter was US$21.35 billion, including US$8.86 billion in wealth management net revenue, US$6.3 billion in equity sales and trading revenue, US$2.46 billion in fixed income, foreign exchange and commodities business sales and trading revenue, US$798 million in consulting revenue, US$851 million in equity underwriting revenue, US$788 million in fixed income underwriting revenue, and earnings per share of US$3.46.

[ASML releases financial report for the second quarter of 2026: net sales reached 9.3 billion euros, raising full-year performance guidance] ASML (ASML) releases its second quarter financial report for 2026.

Data show that the company achieved net sales of 9.3 billion euros in the quarter (8.8 billion euros in the previous quarter), a gross profit margin of 54.0%, net profit of 2.9 billion euros, and basic earnings per share (EPS) of 7.59 euros.

Due to the solid performance in the second quarter, ASML raised its performance guidance for 2026, raising its full-year net sales forecast to between 43 billion and 45 billion euros, and raising its gross profit margin forecast to 54% to 56%. The company also expects net sales in the third quarter to be between 11 billion and 12 billion euros.

In addition, ASML announced that it will pay an interim dividend of 1.88 euros per share for 2026.

[BlackRock’s net profit in the second quarter was US$1.914 billion, a year-on-year increase of 20%] BlackRock announced that revenue in the second quarter of 2026 was US$7.084 billion, a year-on-year increase of 31%; net profit was US$1.914 billion, a year-on-year increase of 20%.

In addition, BlackRock’s asset management scale reached US$15.34 trillion in the second quarter, a year-on-year increase of 22%. [BASF’s sales in the second quarter were 17.2 billion euros] BASF announced its second-quarter performance report.

The report showed that BASF's second-quarter sales were 17.2 billion euros, with an estimate of 16.38 billion euros; adjusted profit before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization was 2.4 billion euros, with an estimate of 2.11 billion euros.

BASF expects full-year adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization to range from 6.9 billion euros to 7.7 billion euros, compared with its previous forecast of 6.2 billion euros to 7 billion euros.

[Alibaba Qianwen will be integrated into Apple Smart as an AI capability] "Kechuangban Daily" exclusively learned that Alibaba Qianwen will be integrated into Apple smart phones as AI capabilities, bringing intelligent experiences to Chinese users of iOS, iPadOS, macOS and visionOS.

Users can directly experience Qianwen's text and image understanding, content generation and other capabilities on Apple devices without switching between applications. Boosted by this news, Alibaba's US stock price rose more than 4% before the market opened.

Today, the Cyberspace Administration of China released registration information for 7 models that provide mobile phone-side generative artificial intelligence services, including Apple Smart, Huawei Xiaoyi model, OPPO AndesGPT model, etc.

[Goldman Sachs launches three-part Eurobond issuance, having previously issued USD bonds to raise US$10 billion] Goldman Sachs Group Inc. reportedly launched a three-part eurobond sale on Wednesday. One day ago, the company released its performance report and completed a US$10 billion bond issuance in the United States.

Goldman Sachs hopes to raise at least 1.5 billion euros ($1.7 billion) in the benchmark-sized deal, a person familiar with the matter said.

The bond is divided into two parts: floating rate and fixed rate, both of which can be redeemed for the first time after three years; there is also a batch of longer-term fixed-rate bonds that can be redeemed after seven years.

[Samsung Electronics plans to outsource Google TPU I/O chip back-end design work] According to reports, due to growing OEM demand, Samsung Electronics is considering outsourcing the back-end design work of Google’s TPU I/O chips.

The company is considering cooperating with three local Korean semiconductor design service companies, AD Technology, Gaonchips, and Alphachips.

[LG New Energy is reported to have supplied batteries to Google’s ultra-large photovoltaic energy storage project in the United States, with a scale of hundreds of billions of won] According to reports, LG New Energy will supply batteries to the ultra-large photovoltaic energy storage system (ESS) project in the United States in which Google participates.

The supply scale is expected to reach hundreds of billions of won. Google announced on the 14th that it will participate in the construction of the largest solar energy project in the United States, with a total scale of 2.5GW.

🚨Oil rises a fourth straight day, with Goldman now warning Brent could top $110 by fall.

-Brent gained 0.4 percent to $85.28, with WTI crossing $80 to $80.02

-The four-day climb tracks the renewed strikes, the blockade, and the strait's disrupted flows

-The new forecast is the headline: Goldman Sachs says Brent could exceed $110 in the fourth quarter if Gulf exports stay constrained

-The fourth quarter ends days before the midterms, putting the projection's timeline directly on top of the election

The Goldman number reframes the entire deadline game.

$110 crude in Q4 means gas prices climbing through the fall campaign, the exact scenario Trump's off-ramp in June was built to prevent, and the exact bet Iran is making by refusing talks. 

Every day of blockade and strikes now trades short-term coercion against fourth-quarter pain, and both capitals can read the same chart. 

The pipelines that make the Gulf strait-proof arrive in 2027. 

The election arrives first.

Just in: Amazing conclusion! JPMorgan: The most pressing issue in the energy market is no longer Hormuz, but Russia.

It is undeniable that as the United States and Iran are once again at war with each other, the situation in the Strait of Hormuz is still the focus of global market attention.

However, as the crack spread, which reflects the difference between the price of crude oil and the price of its refined products, has widened unprecedentedly in recent weeks, some industry professionals are worried that a deeper problem in the energy market may be being overlooked by investors...

As Natasha Kaneva, head of commodity research at JPMorgan Chase, pointed out in the latest "Crude Oil Market Weekly Report", a new round of mutual attacks between the United States and Iran has caused another sharp drop in tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, basically reversing the hard-won recovery momentum in the past few weeks.

However, after months of frequent disruptions, the market has actually become more relaxed when it comes to crude oil supply and tends to view it as a dynamic issue: Crude oil supplies can evaporate instantly and return just as quickly. However, the uncertainty in the refining and chemical sector is much greater right now.

As Kaneva writes, the question is no longer whether crude supplies can be restored, but how quickly the global refining system can process them.

In this regard, there are three main uncertainties: The extent of damage to the Middle East's refining infrastructure after months of conflict; the time it will take for China's refining capacity to be restored; and the speed of Russia's refining system to recover from repeated Ukrainian drone attacks.

On the Middle East front, JPMorgan has tracked more than 30 attacks on various refineries and oil processing infrastructure in the region, but the extent of the actual physical damage remains largely unknown.

The key question is what proportion of the region's 11.7 million barrels per day of refining capacity can be restarted immediately, and what proportion requires extensive maintenance.

In this regard, JPMorgan's baseline forecast is that by the end of this year, only 250,000 barrels per day of production capacity will remain closed, although the bank's confidence in this forecast is relatively low. At the same time, JPMorgan Chase believes that the most underestimated source of uncertainty currently is Russia.

Russia's refining capacity plummets Russia's refining system has been enduring repeated Ukrainian drone attacks over the past three months, which have damaged refineries, storage facilities and, increasingly, the complex secondary conversion units that determine product yields.

Russia's refinery throughput has fallen to 3.8 million barrels per day in June, 1.5 million barrels per day below the level at the beginning of the year, making Russia one of the biggest reasons for the unusually weak global refining activity today.

Data show that global oil refining and processing volume decreased by 8.2 million barrels per day this year, of which Russia alone accounted for about 20%. Domestically, Russia’s fuel supply situation has deteriorated significantly and is increasingly becoming an operational issue, not just a consumer one.

Shortages have spread even to regions with their own refineries as supplies are redirected to Moscow. This reflects a clear shift in targeting in Ukraine: in 2026, refineries supplying oil to Moscow accounted for 39% of all targets, compared with only 22% in 2025.

Fuel shortages are becoming increasingly acute as Russia enters its traditional "dacha" season - when millions of Russians head to their summer villas and seasonal gasoline demand reaches its annual peak. In some areas, drivers waited in line for days, fuel sales were rationed, and gas stations even temporarily ran out of fuel.

Retail prices have risen sharply, with some independent service stations reportedly selling 50% or more above normal levels. The impact of these supply disruptions now extends far beyond private car owners.

Agriculture, public transportation, utilities, logistics and small businesses are being increasingly impacted, signaling a shift from consumer inconvenience to widespread operational disruption across the economy.

Diesel supply issues are even more concerning because agricultural harvesting in Russia's southern regions typically begins in August, when demand for seasonal distillates typically reaches its peak of the year.

In the face of the crisis, the Russian government's current response logic is more focused on ensuring short-term fuel supply security than on substantive repairs of refining capacity.

At present, Russia has begun emergency imports of gasoline from Belarus and India, approved tax amendments aimed at stimulating gasoline production by increasing blending components, and specially approved refineries to produce and sell lower-standard Euro-3 gasoline to replace Euro-5, thereby increasing supply through low-standard blending materials.

As refinery operating rates decline and seasonal demand peaks resonate, Russia has also comprehensively tightened export controls on gasoline, aviation kerosene and diesel.

Xiaomo pointed out that although these measures are pragmatic measures to ensure domestic supply, they also reveal that Russia is currently only able to compensate for fuel shortages at the margin, rather than relying on a fully resilient oil refining system to operate. Will the impact be felt around the world?

JPMorgan predicts the developments could have repercussions far beyond Russia. Unable to process crude oil domestically, Russia will divert more crude oil to the export market, pushing crude oil exports closer to historical highs and exacerbating an already oversupplied crude oil market.

At the same time, the country's refined oil exports will decline sharply. The most affected are middle distillates. Russia's diesel and fuel oil exports fell by about two-thirds, further exacerbating a supply-demand imbalance in an already tight global distillate market and keeping refining margins high.

The reason why this is important is that Russia plays an extremely significant role in the global refined oil trade - it is the world's second largest diesel exporter, supplying approximately 12% of global diesel exports; it is also the largest exporter of fuel oil, with a market share of approximately 16%.

Therefore, disruptions in the Russian refining industry tend to have a far greater impact on the supply and demand balance of refined oil products than on the supply and demand balance of crude oil.

The key question is whether Russia's refining system will eventually return to something close to normal operations or whether it will slip into a less structural equilibrium punctuated by outages.

Before the escalation of drone attacks in early April this year, the normal processing capacity of Russian refineries remained at around 5.3 million barrels per day.

Based on this processing capacity, the system can produce approximately 2 million barrels of diesel, 1 million barrels of gasoline, 900,000 barrels of fuel oil, 300,000 barrels of aviation kerosene and 1.1 million barrels of other refined oil products per day.

Among them, the Russian domestic market consumes most of the gasoline and jet fuel, while nearly half of the diesel and almost all the fuel oil are exported, making it one of the core supply sources of the global distillate market.

Xiaomo pointed out that this normalized operating benchmark is the most effective reference for assessing the depth of current supply disruptions. Currently, the biggest risk to the market is the "persistence" of this shock.

Since late March, Ukraine’s offensive has evolved from sporadic “nuisance attacks” into a premeditated, long-term strategic operation aimed at paralyzing Russia’s entire refining and distribution system.

The frequent attacks, deeper domestic penetration, and wider attack scope all imply that refinery shutdowns are gradually becoming a structural norm in the market, rather than a fleeting temporary disturbance. In addition, Ukraine clearly knows how to hit Russia's "soft underbelly".

Recent attacks are increasingly focused on hydrocracking units, catalytic cracking units, reforming units and other secondary conversion units, and high-frequency "second strikes" are carried out on damaged facilities.

Compared with repairing relatively simple atmospheric distillation units, rebuilding these precision devices is extremely difficult and may directly extend the downtime period from just a few weeks to several quarters. As a result, the refined oil market is tighter than the crude oil market and is very likely to continue until 2027.

JPMorgan's baseline forecast favors a gradual recovery rather than a dramatic and rapid normalization. The bank predicts that the operating rate of Russian refineries will gradually rise from the current 3.6 million barrels per day to about 4.5 million barrels per day in early 2027, with the annual average in 2027 being about 4.7 million barrels per day.

The premise of this prediction is that the intensity of drone attacks will slow down as Ukraine shifts its defense focus back to its own energy infrastructure before the end of Russia's State Duma elections in September and the arrival of the winter heating season.

Even under this scenario, upstream crude oil production is expected to rebound only slightly, as downstream bottlenecks are increasingly affecting upstream investment decisions.

Russia's crude oil production currently remains at 8.7 million barrels per day, a decrease of nearly 600,000 barrels per day from the beginning of the year, which is approaching the actual delivery limit of its crude oil export infrastructure. If refinery utilization cannot be effectively boosted, excess crude oil will have nowhere to put it.

In other words, even though the global market is still naturally focused on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the most critical players in determining the global oil refining balance may have quietly moved to Russia, nearly 2,000 miles north of Hormuz. (

We are half-way through an economic data heavy week and it has been good news so far for inflation readings. Core CPI beat expectations on Tuesday, headline CPI month-over-month was down -0.4%, an improvement from May’s figures which were up 0.5%. Markets responded positively, with E-mini S&P 500 Index Futures rising 0.45% on Tuesday. The index for energy fell -5.7% in June, compared to rising 3.9% in May. Energy was the largest contributor to the monthly, all items decrease and offset increases across other indexes including shelter and food.

The Producer Price Index (PPI) was expected to remain steady in June compared to rising 1.1% for the prior month. Final demand numbers show PPI fell -0.3% in June compared to increasing of 0.6% percent in May.  On an annual basis, the index indicated a 5.5% inflation rate. Excluding food and energy, core PPI rose 0.2%, against the outlook for a 0.3% increase. As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to receding tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

Light Sweet Crude Oil Futures for August (/CLQ26) are higher 0.48% to 79.74 (As of 8:45 AM ET), Wednesday morning after the release of the newest PPI numbers. The E-mini S&P 500 Index Futures for the month of September (/ESU26) moved higher 0.25% to 7610.50 on the news. Despite the win, with the inflation readings, market participants are listening to comments from members of the fed for hints to future interest rate policy decisions. Currently the CME Fed Watch tool shows a 90.9% probability of the target rate holding firm at 350-375 bps for the July 29, Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Looking out to September, however, the story changes and the odds are almost 50/50 for a rate increase. The probability of a 25 bps increase in September is 47%, and something to monitor during the coming weeks.

One of the main drivers behind lower inflation number for June was the ceasefire and the opening of the Straight of Hormuz. Over the month of June, August Crude oil Futures were lower -23.12%. The month of July started with Crude Oil below $70 (68.98 as of open July 1) and now has moved higher 16.4% and pushing up against $80. Over the past couple of weeks, the ceasefire and peace talks have stalled. This morning, the U.S. launched air strikes on Iran. President Donald Trump had warned that strikes would intensify if Tehran does not cooperate in peace talks and added that U.S. forces would target key infrastructure next without a diplomatic breakthrough.

As market participants consider the impact of recent geopolitical escalations against the new data points. Investors will be asking the following questions: Will escalations with Iran, reverse the positive inflation reading we saw in June? 晚间市场报告：鸽派 CPI 撑到收盘，夜里多头开始遇麻烦

白天市场只信「6 月能源便宜」这份过期数据——可油没跌、战争在夜里升级。这份鸽派定价扛过了美股收盘，但明早 05:30 加州时间的 PPI 是第一次复核。

【一、宏观：鸽派 CPI 的涨，扛到了美股收盘】
• 6 月 CPI 全线爆冷（整体环比 -0.4%、核心环比 0%）撑起普涨，美股收盘保住：标普 +0.38%｜纳指 +0.90%｜费城半导体 +2.54%（独强，存储+芯片领涨）
• 10 年美债 4.585%（较昨收 4.609% 下行）｜VIX 16.5（-3.85%）｜加息概率 42% → 17%
• 但夜盘两个反向信号：现货金回落到 $4,036（-0.40%）、且特朗普正在筹划对伊朗更大规模的攻势

📌 白天市场只信「6 月能源便宜」这份过期数据，可油没跌、战争在夜里升级。鸽派定价扛过了收盘，明早 PPI 是第一次复核。

【二、国际形势：战争在夜里升级，不是降级】
• AXIOS（三名知情人士）：特朗普周二在白宫战情室开会，讨论对伊朗发动更大规模攻势，范围将比目前霍尔木兹周边的打击更广
• 伊朗革命卫队袭击了美军在科威特的设施，战线继续扩到海湾
• WTI 原油 $79.42（+0.33%），仍稳在 79 上方——7 月初 71 涨上来后没回去
• 高盛给出极端双向：若海湾出口复苏停滞，Q4 布伦特可能破 $110；若产量超预期、需求复苏慢，年底也可能跌到 $60

📌 供给端坏消息一整天没停，风险资产却全靠一份 6 月 CPI 撑着。断掉的是「油 → 通胀」这一环，而且很可能只是延后。油没跌 + 战在升级 = 明早 PPI 和 8 月那份 CPI 才是真审判。

【三、技术面：超买消化，趋势没破】
• BTC $64,525（+3.23%）｜24h 高 65,100 / 低 62,286
• ETH $1,867（+4.83%）｜24h 高 1,898 / 低 1,773
• SOL $77.29（+3.11%）｜最弱

• 1 小时 RSI 从早上的 76 降到 62 / 61 → 超买被时间消化掉了（回踩没破位，是健康的）
• 4 小时均线多头排列，三币全站上日线 MA50
• 但三个币仍然全部在日线 MA200 下方 → 这是熊市反弹，不是趋势反转

⭐ 关键位：BTC 今早突破的 64,700 没守住，成了日内轴心；ETH 收复日线 MA50（1,747）是三个里最干净的一条。

【四、衍生品：ETH 仍是唯一有真新钱的那个】
🔴 BTC 持仓量：4 小时 -0.47%，24 小时 +1.78%（全天只是小幅回补，不是新趋势）
🟢 ETH 持仓量：4 小时 -1.6%，24 小时 +9.26%（真的有新多头，主战场还在 ETH）

• 美国现货相对全球盘仍是折价 -0.0851% / -$55（比午间的 -$41 还走阔）→ 美国现货买盘依然缺席，BTC 这波不是美资在推
• 资金费率 BTC/ETH 均 +0.01%（温和，没有拥挤）
• 恐惧贪婪指数 26（恐惧区，早上 22）——价格涨了一天，情绪还在地板上
• 成交量：ETH 永续 24h $83.9 亿 ＞ BTC $74.0 亿（ETH 继续反超）

📌 结论不变：要做多，做 ETH，不做 BTC。

【五、美股与存储：收盘保住涨幅，但基本面冒出新变量】
美股收盘（真实日涨跌，已排除前收陷阱）：
🟢 美光 MU $983.12　+4.92%
🟢 闪迪 SNDK $1,757.82　+5.01%
🟡 SPCX $136.08（-2.20%，发行价 135，勉强守住没破发）

永续合约（夜盘继续走强）：SK 海力士韩股腿 $1,424｜ADR 腿 $188.38｜MU $992.92｜SNDK $1,781.75

⭐ SK 海力士 ADR 相对韩股溢价仍在 ~32%（部分口径接近 50%）。7/29 韩国新股上市前，套利通道物理关闭，溢价无强制收敛机制。

⚡ 今晚冒出的新基本面变量（偏利多存储，做空要小心）：
• SK 海力士加速为英伟达量产 HBM4
• 美银：海力士 2028 年新增产能或仅为计划的六分之一（供给紧）

📌 韩股腿夜盘 24h +19%，是在提前定价韩国周三跳空高开。存储现在是「拥挤」和「真需求」两股力同时在场——不是单边。

【六、综合判断：白天 CPI 赢了，夜里多头开始遇到麻烦】
🟢 主线（白天）：鸽派 CPI 撑起普涨并扛到收盘，指数收绿、费半独强、存储 +5%
🔴 反向变量（夜里）：① 特朗普筹划对伊朗更大攻势 ② 油没跌仍在 79.4 ③ 金回落 -0.4%——三个都在给多头找麻烦

加密内部：
• BTC 质量仍不合格：现货折价走阔 + 持仓量只小幅回补 + 还在 MA200 下方
• ETH 是唯一有真买盘的品种，但现价 RSI 62，追进去性价比差
• 存储：拥挤度极高 + 基本面利多同时存在 → 别裸多追高，也别裸空硬扛

🧩 本质：这是一个「靠一份过期数据维持的乐观」。它能不能延续，明早 PPI 说了算。

【七、今日交易建议】
🥇 ETH——唯一有真买盘，但别追现价
• 现价 1,867，1 小时 RSI 62，不追
• 回踩 1,800–1,830 是接多区
• 止损 1,745（跌破 = 日线 MA50 收复失败，论点作废）
• 目标 1,950–2,000｜仓位不超过三分之一

🚫 BTC——不做多
盯 64,700 那根 4 小时 K 线能不能收回上方；收不回来今早的突破就是假的，失效位 63,400。

⚖️ 存储——别追高，方向要挑腿
• 一个都别追现价（MU 唯一资金费率≈0，真要多只做它、且等回踩 930）
• 要空，挑「多头付封顶费率」的韩股腿（空这条你收 funding），别裸空 35–44% 溢价的 ADR 腿（7/29 前不收敛）
• 提醒：HBM4 / 英伟达这条真需求线，是空存储的最大对手盘

💡 现金进 PPI 依然是完全合理的一手。

【八、风险事件】
🔥🔥 明早 05:30 加州时间 — 美国 6 月 PPI：「CPI 鸽派能不能站住」的第一次复核。PPI 能源传导更直接，不配合的话，今天这场靠 CPI 撑起来的普涨会立刻反转。
🔥 夜间尾部 — 特朗普对伊朗更大规模攻势（AXIOS）：范围比霍尔木兹周边更广，一旦落地会同时冲击油价和风险偏好。
📅 周三：韩国央行利率决议（预期加息至 2.75%，直接影响海力士韩股腿定价）｜中国 Q2 GDP 已出 +4.3%（较一季度 5.0% 放缓，主因石化受油价拖累）
⚠️ 最大尾部仍是油：「战争 → 油 → 通胀 → 加息 → 杀风险资产」的链条被一份 6 月 CPI 暂时切断，但油没跌、战在升级。断的是中间那一环，很可能只是延后，不是消失。

Nigeria’s Crude Oil Production Hits 74-Month High, Exceeds OPEC Quota Again

Nigeria’s crude oil production rose to an average of 1.56 million barrels per day (bpd) in June 2026, the highest monthly crude oil output since April 2020, according to the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC).

The country produced 104% of its OPEC quota of 1.5 million bpd for the second consecutive month, while combined crude oil and condensate production increased to 1.735 million bpd, marking the fourth consecutive month of production growth.

The NUPRC attributed the improved performance to enhanced pipeline security, stable operations across producing assets, the absence of major pipeline disruptions, and improved production efficiency.

The sustained rise in output strengthens Nigeria’s position as Africa’s leading crude oil producer and is expected to bolster government revenue, foreign exchange earnings, and overall economic growth.
